spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.spec: §0 (i) cardinality argument for H_1: '|W(B_2)|=8 finite vs |W((2A_1)^{(1)})|=∞'
spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.actual: Okamoto slot 07 §2.1 explicitly states G ≅ W^{aff}(B_2), the AFFINE Weyl group, NOT the finite W(B_2). |G| = ∞.
spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.resolution: Phase B reframes the construction at the affine Weyl-group level. The H_1 cardinality argument no longer rules out isomorphism a priori; instead we find an INJECTIVE φ of index 2 (not isomorphism). The spec's H_3 ('quotient mod translation lattice') is replaced by the cleaner H_3* (φ injective, image is index-2 subgroup; cokernel Z/2 is generated by an external Bäcklund involution).

spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.spec: §2 PHASE B.1 default guess H_3: 'classical Weyl quotient W(B_2) ≅ W((2A_1)^{(1)}) / Q^∨ = Z/2 × Z/2 ⋊ Z/2 = D_4'
spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.actual: W(B_2) = D_4 is correct. But the Sakai-side classical quotient is W((2A_1)^{(1)}) / Q^∨ = (Z/2)² (NOT D_4). The D_4 ≅ W(B_2) on the Sakai side comes from H = ⟨σ, ππ'⟩ ⊂ Aut(D_6^{(1)}) (a (Z/2)² subgroup of D_4 = Aut(D_6^{(1)})), combined with the (Z/2)² classical Weyl gives 16, not the desired 8.
spec_vs_construction_discrepancies.resolution: We avoid the 'finite quotient = D_4 on both sides' framing. Instead, the index-2 obstruction is at the level of Aut(D_6^{(1)}) (we capture only H = ⟨σ, ππ'⟩, half of Aut). This is documented in C.2.
